html:
An Eggplant is an uncommon Vegetable that can be farmed from Basic Farms and Improved Farms by planting Seeds for a 11% chance of growing or using Eggplant Seeds (Swirly Seeds). It can be used as an ingredient in several Crock Pot recipes. Feeding an Eggplant to a bird in a Birdcage will produce 1–2 Eggplant Seeds, as well as a 50% chance to get a plain seed. In Don't Starve Together, you will always get 1 Eggplant Seed.

Contents

  • 1 Braised Eggplant
  • 2 Usage
  • 3 Recipes
  • 4 Gallery

Braised Eggplant

Braised Eggplant is a Vegetable Food item made by Cooking an Eggplant on a fire (Campfire, Fire Pit, or a Dwarf Star). It restores much more health than its raw counterpart. It can also be used as an ingredient in several Crock Pot recipes. In most circumstances it is far more efficient to cook the Eggplant, as a Braised Eggplant heals 12 more health and can regenerate spoilage time.

Usage

×3

×4

×4

Recipes

×2

Cannot include Mandrake

Gallery

  • Original HD Eggplant icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Original HD Eggplant Cooked icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Icon used as emoji for Official Klei Discord

“It doesn't look like an egg.”
–Wilson, when examining an Eggplant.
“Definitely not a bird.”
–Willow, when examining an Eggplant.
“Is not egg!”
–Wolfgang, when examining an Eggplant.
“Almost a deadly nightshade. Almost.”
–Wendy, when examining an Eggplant.
“IT HAS AN ILLOGICAL NAME”
–WX-78, when examining an Eggplant.
“A nightshade, specifically Solanum Melongena.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining an Eggplant.
“I bet birds have something to do with this.”
–Woodie, when examining an Eggplant.
“Just another boring plant.”
–Maxwell, when examining an Eggplant.
“I can reliably predict this will upset my stomach.”
–Wagstaff, when examining an Eggplant.
“Purpley and bulböus.”
–Wigfrid, when examining an Eggplant.
“Blech, eggplant.”
–Webber, when examining an Eggplant.
“I've never seen an egg that shade of purple.”
–Walani, when examining an Eggplant.
“Aubergine!”
–Warly, when examining an Eggplant.
“She's a nice shade o' purple.”
–Woodlegs, when examining an Eggplant.
“'TIS PURPLEY NUM NUMS”
–Wilba, when examining an Eggplant.
“Mmm...”
–Wormwood, when examining an Eggplant.
“The best of the purple vegetables, if you ask me.”
–Wheeler, when examining an Eggplant.
“Look how weird it is! Ha!”
–Winona, when examining an Eggplant.
“I've never seen such a thing before!”
–Wortox, when examining an Eggplant.
“Big purple yummy!”
–Wurt, when examining an Eggplant.
“Even in the wilderness you have to eat your vegetables.”
–Walter, when examining an Eggplant.
“It kind of looks like a giant jellybean if you squint.”
–Wanda, when examining an Eggplant.
An Eggplant is an uncommon Vegetable that can be farmed from Basic Farms and Improved Farms by planting Seeds for a 11% chance of growing or using Eggplant Seeds (Swirly Seeds). It can be used as an ingredient in several Crock Pot recipes. Feeding an Eggplant to a bird in a Birdcage will produce 1–2 Eggplant Seeds, as well as a 50% chance to get a plain seed. In Don't Starve Together, you will always get 1 Eggplant Seed.

1 Braised Eggplant
2 Usage
3 Recipes
4 Gallery
Braised Eggplant[edit | edit source]
“It's even less eggy.”
–Wilson, when examining a Braised Eggplant.
“Using fire on it made it a tastier eggplant.”
–Willow, when examining a Braised Eggplant.
“Has no yolk! Yolk is strongest part!”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Braised Eggplant.
“Cooking this nightshade made it even less deadly.”
–Wendy, when examining a Braised Eggplant.
“FANCY”
–WX-78, when examining a Braised Eggplant.
“I present: Braised Solanum Melongena.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Braised Eggplant.
“Suspiciously birdy.”
–Woodie, when examining a Braised Eggplant.
“A little more refined, at least.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Braised Eggplant.
“Braising is scientifically proven to settle my stomach.”
–Wagstaff, when examining a Braised Eggplant.
“Fööd för the weak.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Braised Eggplant.
“Mum knew I liked it braised.”
–Webber, when examining a Braised Eggplant.
“Is this, like, an omelette?”
–Walani, when examining a Braised Eggplant.
“Could use tomato sauce and Parmesan...”
–Warly, when examining a Braised Eggplant.
“Look whut Woodlegs cooked.”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Braised Eggplant.
“'TIS FWOOSH'DED PURPLEY NUM NUMS”
–Wilba, when examining a Braised Eggplant.
“Belly likes it”
–Wormwood, when examining a Braised Eggplant.
“I prefer my eggplants scrambled.”
–Wheeler, when examining a Braised Eggplant.
“Did that make it better? I don't know.”
–Winona, when examining a Braised Eggplant.
“My favorite treat... it can't be beat!”
–Wortox, when examining a Braised Eggplant.
“Brings out purple flavor, flort.”
–Wurt, when examining a Braised Eggplant.
“Oh, it's much better cooked!”
–Walter, when examining a Braised Eggplant.
“Well, the illusion is ruined.”
–Wanda, when examining a Braised Eggplant.
Braised Eggplant is a Vegetable Food item made by Cooking an Eggplant on a fire (Campfire, Fire Pit, or a Dwarf Star). It restores much more health than its raw counterpart. It can also be used as an ingredient in several Crock Pot recipes. In most circumstances it is far more efficient to cook the Eggplant, as a Braised Eggplant heals 12 more health and can regenerate spoilage time.

An '''Eggplant''' is an uncommon [[:Category:Vegetables|Vegetable]] that can be [[Farming|farmed]] from [[Basic Farm]]s and [[Improved Farm]]s by planting [[Seeds]] for a 11% chance of growing or using [[Crop Seeds|Eggplant Seeds]] (Swirly Seeds). It can be used as an ingredient in several [[Crock Pot]] recipes. Feeding an Eggplant to a bird in a [[Birdcage]] will produce 1–2 Eggplant Seeds, as well as a 50% chance to get a plain seed. In [[Don't Starve Together]], you will always get 1 [[Crop Seeds|Eggplant Seed]].

==Braised Eggplant==

'''Braised Eggplant''' is a [[:Category:Vegetables|Vegetable]] [[Food]] item made by [[Cooking]] an Eggplant on a fire ([[Campfire]], [[Fire Pit]], or a [[Star Caller's Staff|Dwarf Star]]). It restores much more [[health]] than its raw counterpart. It can also be used as an ingredient in several Crock Pot recipes. In most circumstances it is far more efficient to cook the Eggplant, as a Braised Eggplant heals 12 more health and can regenerate spoilage time.
